name: Driver Attrition
example: FL carrier driver count down 18% in 90 days, no fleet reduction filed
bridge: Staffing and insurance gaps follow

name: Authority Milestone
example: New authority filed in 3 states (CA, AZ, NV) — first interstate expansion
bridge: Scaling carrier needs new vendors fast

name: Safety Score Deterioration
example: Unsafe Driving BASIC jumped from 42% to 71% in 6 months
bridge: Compliance overhaul and insurance re-rating incoming

name: Insurance Lapse
example: BIPD coverage lapsed 14 days ago, no replacement filing on record
bridge: Authority revocation risk — needs a new policy now

name: Fleet Size Change
example: Power units increased from 12 to 38 in 90 days
bridge: More trucks means more drivers, fuel, insurance, maintenance

name: New Carrier Authority
example: New MC authority granted last week, 0 inspections — greenfield carrier
bridge: Needs 13 vendors on day one

name: Crash Spike
example: 3 DOT-reportable crashes in 60 days vs. 1 in prior 12 months
bridge: Insurance re-rating and compliance scrutiny ahead

name: Out-of-Service Rate Spike
example: Vehicle OOS rate hit 34% across last 8 inspections — national avg is 21%
bridge: Maintenance or compliance failure — needs help now

name: Permit Volume Spike
example: Carrier pulled 47 trip permits in March vs. 12 avg
bridge: New contract or route expansion underway

name: Oversize/Overweight Permits
example: First-time OS/OW permits filed in 3 states — entering heavy haul
bridge: Higher-value loads, higher-risk operations

name: Commercial Build Permit
example: 15,000 sqft commercial build permit filed in industrial zone
bridge: New facility means new vendors across the board

name: Renovation Surge
example: 4 permits filed in 60 days: electrical, plumbing, HVAC, structural
bridge: Major upgrade — repositioning or expanding capacity

name: Solar / EV Install Permit
example: Commercial solar array permit filed — 200kW system, $340K estimated value
bridge: Infrastructure investment signals growth mindset

name: Contractor Activity Spike
example: Contractor pulled 23 permits in Q1 vs. 8 avg — pipeline is loaded
bridge: Growing fast, likely needs labor and capital

name: New Address Permit
example: Known HVAC company filing first permit at new address 40 miles from HQ
bridge: Expanding territory — new market entry

name: Demolition Permit
example: Demo permit for 8,000 sqft retail space in downtown Austin
bridge: Redevelopment pipeline incoming

name: Revenue Acceleration
example: Card revenue growth 42% above seasonal baseline — breakout trajectory
bridge: Ready to buy, hire, or borrow

name: Revenue Decline
example: 12-month avg card revenue down 28% YoY, transaction count stable
bridge: Margin compression — open to cost optimization

name: New Location Detected
example: Second location in Austin showing first card transactions this month
bridge: Physical expansion confirmed — needs infrastructure

name: Transaction Volume Surge
example: Monthly transactions up 3x in 90 days, unique customers doubled
bridge: Operational capacity is about to be strained

name: Seasonal Pre-Ramp
example: Landscaping co. revenue peaks Jun–Sep every year — it's March
bridge: Outreach window before they're slammed

name: Business Closure Risk
example: Active LLC but 0 card revenue for 6 months, website down, no reviews
bridge: Ghost business — clean your pipeline

name: Payment Processor Change
example: Average transaction size shifted from $22 to $67 overnight
bridge: New infrastructure — open to new vendors

name: Multi-Location Growth
example: 3 new business locations showing transactions in last 120 days
bridge: Franchise or chain scaling — needs everything at volume

name: New Entity Formation
example: LLC formed 3 weeks ago in Delaware with registered agent in TX
bridge: New venture — needs vendors across the board

name: DBA / Trade Name Filed
example: Existing logistics LLC filed DBA 'FastFreight Express'
bridge: New brand or service line launching

name: Foreign Qualification
example: TX-based company filed foreign qualification in FL, GA, NC
bridge: Multi-state expansion confirmed

name: Agent / Officer Change
example: CEO replaced on annual report filing — new name, same entity
bridge: New leadership, new vendor relationships

name: Reinstatement After Dissolution
example: LLC reinstated after 14 months dissolved — back on the registry
bridge: Back in business, starting from scratch on vendors

name: New UCC Lien Filed
example: UCC-1 filed by equipment finance co. — $200K+ asset acquisition likely
bridge: Capital being deployed — business is investing

name: UCC Termination
example: 3-year equipment lien terminated — debt fully paid off
bridge: Refinance or new lender acquisition window open

name: OSHA Inspection Spike
example: 2 OSHA inspections in 90 days with serious citations issued
bridge: Compliance overhaul needed — budget unlocking

name: OSHA Penalty Issued
example: $45K in OSHA penalties for fall protection violations
bridge: Safety budget just got unlocked

name: Hiring Surge
example: Posted 12 roles in 2 weeks after 0 for 6 months
bridge: Scaling fast, buying everything

name: Leadership Role Posted
example: VP of Operations posted — role open for 3 days
bridge: New decision-maker incoming — vendor review follows

name: Layoff Signal
example: 40 active listings pulled in 1 week, no new posts
bridge: Restructuring — cost optimization vendors get the call

name: Tech Stack Adoption
example: First job posting mentioning Snowflake — new data infrastructure
bridge: New tool means new budget, new integrations needed

name: Tech Stack Abandonment
example: Stopped mentioning Salesforce in job postings after 2 years
bridge: CRM migration likely — replacement vendor window

name: Funding Announced
example: Series B announced at $18M — hiring, tooling, expansion ahead
bridge: Budget just got unlocked across every department

name: Acquisition / Merger
example: Acquired competitor with 200 employees — integration underway
bridge: Every vendor relationship gets reviewed

name: Executive Departure
example: CTO departed after 4 years — tech stack and vendor review window open
bridge: Vendor relationships back on the table

name: Regulatory Action
example: EPA consent decree issued — $2M remediation required
bridge: Compliance spend and consulting needs spike

name: Commercial Lease Filed
example: 10-year lease recorded for 25,000 sqft warehouse
bridge: Major operational expansion committed

name: Property Tax Assessment Change
example: Commercial property assessment up 40% — major improvements completed
bridge: Facility investment done — operational scaling next

name: New License Application
example: Liquor license application filed for new address — restaurant or bar opening
bridge: New venue needs insurance, staffing, POS, payroll

name: License Renewal Lapse
example: Health department license expired 45 days ago, no renewal filed

name: SEC Filing Event
example: Form D filed — private placement underway, growth capital incoming

name: Government Contract Award
example: $3.2M DOD contract awarded — guaranteed revenue for 3 years
bridge: Capacity expansion needed to fulfill

name: Bankruptcy Filing
example: Chapter 11 filed — restructuring with intent to continue operations
bridge: New vendor relationships forming under new structure

name: Active Litigation
example: Named defendant in $5M breach of contract suit — filed last month
bridge: Legal spend rising, risk posture shifting

name: Ad Spend Increase
example: LinkedIn ad spend up 4x in 30 days — first Google ads appeared
bridge: Growth mode — investing in acquisition

name: Platform Migration
example: Shopify store migrated to BigCommerce — new app stack needed
bridge: Entire vendor ecosystem in flux

name: Ecommerce Revenue Spike
example: Estimated monthly sales jumped from $40K to $180K in one quarter
bridge: Outgrowing current infrastructure

name: New Practice License
example: New medical practice license issued — internal medicine, solo practitioner
bridge: New practice needs EHR, billing, staffing, insurance credentialing

name: CMS Certification Change
example: Facility gained Medicare/Medicaid certification — new patient pool accessible
bridge: Revenue stream unlocked — scaling to meet demand

name: Healthcare Facility Permit
example: Permit filed for 12,000 sqft medical office buildout

name: HIPAA Violation
example: OCR settlement for $180K — data breach affecting 12,000 patients
bridge: Compliance remediation and IT security spend incoming

name: Provider Headcount Change
example: NPI registry shows 4 new providers added in 60 days — was stable for 2 years
bridge: Practice scaling — operational infrastructure needs to catch up

name: Health Inspection Failure
example: Critical violations on health inspection — score dropped from 96 to 71
bridge: Remediation required — compliance and consulting spend ahead

name: Hotel / Lodging Permit
example: Short-term rental permit filed — 8-unit property converting to boutique hotel

name: Zoning Change Approved
example: Parcel rezoned from residential to mixed-use commercial — development eligible

name: Building Sale Recorded
example: $4.2M commercial property sale recorded — new owner, new plans
bridge: Ownership change triggers vendor reset

name: Vacancy Rate Spike
example: Office building occupancy dropped from 89% to 62% in 6 months
bridge: Repositioning or tenant acquisition push coming

name: Manufacturing Equipment Filing
example: UCC filing on $1.2M CNC machining center — production capacity expanding
bridge: Capex deployed — hiring and maintenance follow

name: EPA Violation
example: Clean Water Act violation — discharge permit exceedance at main facility
bridge: Environmental compliance spend unlocking

name: Product Recall
example: CPSC recall issued — 45,000 units affected, replacement program required
bridge: Supply chain, legal, and PR spend all spike

name: New Retail Distribution
example: Brand first appeared on Target.com — previously DTC only
bridge: Channel expansion — logistics, packaging, staffing needs change

name: Amazon Seller Rank Spike
example: Best Seller Rank jumped from 45,000 to 800 in 30 days — breakout product

name: New Trademark Filed
example: Trademark application filed for new brand name — product line expansion
bridge: New brand launching — marketing, packaging, distribution ahead

name: New Office Lease
example: Professional services firm signed 5-year lease on 8,000 sqft — doubling footprint
bridge: Physical expansion means headcount growth is committed

example: Named partner departed — firm filing amended with state bar

example: New CPA firm license issued — 3 partners, first year of operations

example: Medical malpractice suit filed — $2.5M claim against surgical group

example: New food service establishment permit — fast-casual concept, 3,200 sqft
